telegram_rate_limit_issue.root_cause: telegram-notify.py script in dispatch.yaml Pipeline start step hitting Telegram API rate limits
telegram_rate_limit_issue.error_message: Error sending message: 429 Client Error: Too Many Requests for url: https://api.telegram.org/bot***/sendMessage
telegram_rate_limit_issue.resolution_note: Telegram rate limits have cleared. Sampled repos (www.feeditout.com, pass-gnome-extension, redhatter) now passing on main branch.
telegram_rate_limit_issue.impact: Was causing CICD workflow to fail at Pipeline start, skipping all subsequent steps (lint, test, build, publish)
telegram_rate_limit_issue.fix_recommendation: For future: Consider implementing rate limit handling in telegram-notify.py to prevent similar issues.

open_dependabot_prs.title: Bump minimatch from 3.1.2 to 3.1.5
open_dependabot_prs.author: dependabot[bot]
open_dependabot_prs.issue: GNOME publish step fails with missing credentials
open_dependabot_prs.fix_description: The gnome-extension-publisher requires GNOME_USERNAME and GNOME_PASSWORD secrets which are not distributed by setup.py. Error: 'Username and password are required for upload.'
open_dependabot_prs.action_recommendation: Add GNOME_USERNAME and GNOME_PASSWORD to setup.py secret distribution, then rerun workflow

open_dependabot_prs.title: chore(deps): bump pypdf from 6.7.5 to 6.8.0
open_dependabot_prs.author: dependabot[bot]
open_dependabot_prs.issue: Missing Telegram secrets on dependabot PR branch (expected)
open_dependabot_prs.fix_description: Dependabot PRs don't have access to secrets by default in GitHub Actions. Pipeline start step requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID which are empty. This is a GitHub security feature, not a bug.
open_dependabot_prs.action_recommendation: Cannot auto-merge due to secret access restrictions. Owner can manually merge if dependency update is safe.

gnome_extension_publisher.issue: GNOME extension publishing requires credentials not in setup.py
gnome_extension_publisher.root_cause: The gnome-extension-publisher tool requires GNOME_USERNAME and GNOME_PASSWORD secrets for uploading to extensions.gnome.org. These secrets are not in the current setup.py distribution list.
gnome_extension_publisher.fix_required: Add GNOME_USERNAME and GNOME_PASSWORD to setup.py secret distribution
gnome_extension_publisher.fix_implementation: Add two new secrets to the SECRETS list and obtain credentials from GNOME Extensions account
gnome_extension_publisher.impact: Blocks GNOME extension publishing across all repos with gnome-extension-publisher enabled

dependabot_telegram_secrets.issue: Dependabot PRs don't have access to repository secrets
dependabot_telegram_secrets.root_cause: GitHub security feature - dependabot PRs run with restricted permissions and cannot access secrets like TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN. This prevents the Pipeline start step from sending notifications.
dependabot_telegram_secrets.fix_required: This is expected behavior and cannot be 'fixed' - it's a GitHub security feature
dependabot_telegram_secrets.fix_implementation: Owner must manually review and merge dependabot PRs if the dependency update is safe. Alternatively, configure workflows to skip notification steps for dependabot branches.
dependabot_telegram_secrets.impact: Prevents auto-merging of dependabot PRs due to Pipeline start failures. Does not affect actual code quality or security.
